### Post by abhiroopb on 2007-11-25
Specs of computer:

1.6ghz Intel Core Duo
100gb HD
1gb RAM
256MB Nvidia geforece go 7400

Laptop LCD Monitor: 15.4" 1280x800
Samsung LCD Monitor: 19" 1440x900

Following is my CURRENT xorg.conf file (created using nvidia-settings)

```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Wed Sep 12 14:30:30 PDT 2007

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon Apr 16 20:37:13 PDT 2007

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "v4l"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

	# generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

	# generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "SAMSUNG LCD"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 81.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 75.0
    Gamma           1
    ModeLine       "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x768@60" 80.1 1280 1344 1480 1680 768 769 772 795 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x720@60" 74.5 1280 1336 1472 1664 720 721 724 746 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x800@60" 83.5 1280 1344 1480 1680 800 801 804 828 -hsync +vsync
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

 # 
    Identifier     "monitor1"
    Gamma           1
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce Go 7400"
EndSection

Section "Device"

 # 
    Identifier     "device1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    BoardName      "nv"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Screen"

# Removed Option "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +1440+0"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +1024+0"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +1440+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"

 # 
    Identifier     "screen1"
    Device         "device1"
    Monitor        "monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
EndSection

```

I personally have no idea what most of the things mean here but its safe to say that my dual monitors are working without any major problems. In fact I managed to sort out the black windows problem using the following:

```


    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"

```

Physically I have the external Samsung LCD in front of me, and the Laptops LCD is to the right.

I have the following problems:

1. I want my laptop's LCD to be the default monitor BUT I want to retain the current orientation (i.e. Samsung in front, Laptop on the right). Unfortunately using nvidia-settings and TwinView whichever monitor is on the left is the default, I would like to change this. The problem with the default monitor is that when I unplug the Samsung LCD from the laptops VGA port to take with me to Uni the laptop screen shows half the screen as though everything else is cut. I then need to go into the graphics option and change the screen res to 1280x800.

2. I used the following thread:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=416249](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=416249)
to be able to play full-screen games. I changed my "metamodes" section from this:
```

   Option         "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +1440+0"

```

to this:
```

   Option         "metamodes" "1440x900,1280x800; 1280x800,NULL"

```

I did this all according to what the guy in the thread said.

I had two problems with this "solution:
    
   a)  Although the resolution of the Samsung was at 1440x900, the screen seemed a bit fuzzy as if the refresh rate was a bit off.
  
   b) I'm not sure why but while playing Nexuiz the game ran VERY slowly at the lowest graphics settings. I don't have the best system but I have played games at higher resolutions than 800x600.

BUT the game was playing on 1 single screen instead of being spread over between the Samsung and the laptop monitor.

Finally, I don't understand what a lot of the commands in the file are, so there may be excess stuff. It would be nice if someone could take a look and perhaps explain it to me and help me clean it up, while at the same time incorporating these other things.

Regards,
